column1: # HDIC is an abbreviation of the Integrated Database of Hanzi Dictionaries in Early Japan
# Ruiju myōgishō 類聚名義抄, Zushoryō manuscript 図書寮本, original edition 原撰本. anonymous Buddhist monk, about 1100.
# Copyright (c) 2026 HDIC project, IKEDA Shoju (Chair, Professor Emeritus, Hokkaido University)
# Text creation and proofreading: IKEDA Shoju and Shin Woongchul
# Data License Information and Access Rights
# Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License (CC BY-SA 4.0)
# This dataset is provided under the Creative Commons Attribution-ShareAlike 4.0 International License (CC BY-SA 4.0).
# - **Attribution (BY):** When using this dataset, you must appropriately attribute the creator/provider of this dataset.
# - **ShareAlike (SA):** If you modify this dataset or create a derivative work based on it, you must release the resulting work under the same CC BY-SA 4.0 license as the original dataset.
# When citing the use, please include '平安時代漢字字書総合データベース (HDIC)', the file name, and the version.
# Example: "Source: 平安時代漢字字書総合データベース (HDIC), ZRM.tsv 1.1.1 (Last update: 21 May 2026)"
# Header Row: The line immediately following these comment lines is the header row containing column names.
# Column Description: Descriptions of the data fields in each row.
# entry_id  An ID uniquely identifying an item. It consists of the letter 'Z' followed by a 6-digit number (e.g., Z004210). These 6 digits encode location and item type information as follows:
#   - Digits 1-3 (ppp): Page number in the facsimile edition (e.g., 004 for page 4).
#   - Digit 4 (r): Row number within the page (e.g., 2 for row 2).
#   - Digit 5 (c): Column number or 段番号 within the row (e.g., 1 for column 1).
#   - Digit 6 (t): Item type (項目種別). This digit indicates the nature of the entry in the manuscript. Possible values are:
#     - 2: Internal addition type 1 (項目内補入項目 タイプ1)
#     - 3: Internal addition type 2 (項目内補入項目 タイプ2)
#   Example: Z004210 indicates a normal entry located on page 4, row 2, column 1 of the manuscript.
# radical_index  The number assigned to the radical. Ranging from 41 to 60, with 48 and 53 being missing. # Corrected spelling from radical_index

# radical_name  The kanji name for the corresponding radical. Shows the kanji corresponding to the radical number. Covers the 18 radicals from number 41 to 60 (水・冫・言・足・立・豆・卜・山・石・玉・邑・阜・土・心・巾・糸・衣). Note that the main text is missing for number 48 (面) and 53 (色).
# page  The page number in the facsimile edition.

# entry The collated kanji. Generally adopts Kangxi Dictionary glyphs, but new glyphs in Unicode (common/popular forms) may also be used. The omission symbol '｜' is replaced with the corresponding character.

# definition    The "Chumon" (notes) regarding the item. Consists of glyph notes, pronunciation notes, meaning notes, Wakun (和訓), and others. The entered glyphs are generally those included in the Kangxi Dictionary.
# url   The Internet public URL of the facsimile image for the corresponding page in the manuscript.
# remarks   Notes by the editor, indicating additional context or supplementary information. # Added back the remarks column description
# Data Conventions: Rules and representations used in the transcription.
# - Empty fields are represented by an empty string (""). (Add other general conventions if applicable, e.g., handling of tabs/newlines within fields).
# - Tone marks (声点) applied to Kanji and Kana are represented by characters:
#   - Additional marks applied with voice points are appended: V for 双点 (indicating 濁音/voiced sound), N for ✓ (indicating 鼻音/nasal sound). E.g., LV, HN.
# - Okototen (ヲコト点) applied to Kanji are replaced with corresponding Hiragana characters.
# - Punctuation marks used are Japanese comma "、" and Japanese period "。".
# - Gōfu (合符), indicating a two-Kanji compound word, are represented by "-" for the middle mark and "_" for the left mark.
# - Kaeriten (返点) are indicated within parentheses, e.g., （㆒）.

# - In the 'definition' field (Chumon), the omission symbol "｜" (indicating the same character as the headword) is represented by "ー" followed by the corresponding character in parentheses, e.g., ー（字）. (Note: In the 'entry' field, this symbol is replaced by the actual character as described in the 'entry' column description).
# - In the 'definition' field, small characters written in double lines (双行) are enclosed in angle brackets < >.

# - Parentheses （ ） in the 'definition' field are retained as they appear in the current working data. They may contain notes on additions, layout, scribal features, source-related comments, or other editorial annotations.

# - Square brackets 【 】 in the 'definition' field are also retained as they appear in the current working data. They are mainly used for editorial or verification notes in the present stage of data preparation.

# - These parenthetical and bracketed notes may be separated into dedicated annotation fields or a separate annotation table in a future revision, but in the current version they are preserved in the 'definition' field to avoid losing information.
# This transcription is primarily based on the digital facsimile images provided by the Archives and Mausolea Department of the Imperial Household Agency (listed below as Sources 1 and 5/6).
# 1.  English: **Collection of the Archives and Mausolea Department, Imperial Household Agency:** Ruiju Myōgishō (box 503, no.212, photographic images).
#    Japanese: **宮内庁書陵部藏:** 類聚名義抄 (box 503, no.212, photographic images).
# 2.  English: **Zushoryōbon Ruiju Myōgishō**, Imperial Household Agency, Archives and Mausolea Department, 1950.
#    Japanese: **圖書寮本類聚名義抄**, 宮内庁書陵部, 1950.
#    Digital access via National Diet Library Digital Collections:
# 3.  English: **Zushoryōbon Ruiju Myōgishō:** In the Collection of the Archives and Mausolea Department, Imperial Household Agency, Benseisha, 1969.
#    Japanese: **図書寮本類聚名義抄 :** 宮内庁書陵部蔵, 勉誠社, 1969.
# 4.  English: **Zushoryōbon Ruiju Myōgishō:** In the Collection of the Archives and Mausolea Department, Imperial Household Agency, Benseisha, 1976, Text Volume, Commentary and Index Volume.
#    Japanese: **図書寮本類聚名義抄 :** 宮内庁書陵部蔵, 勉誠社, 1976, 本文篇 , 解説索引篇.
# 5.  English: **Zushoryōbon Ruiju Myōgishō: Main Text Facsimile, Commentary and Index**, Bensei Publishing, 2005.
#    Japanese: **図書寮本類聚名義抄 : 本文影印 解説索引**, 勉誠出版, 2005.
#    Digital Facsimile available from the Archives and Mausolea Department:

# 6.  English: **Entry in the Archives and Mausolea Department Catalog and Image Disclosure System:** Ruiju Myōgishō (Hō (first half), fragments/lacunae, different edition/version), Imperial Household Agency, 2025.
#    Japanese: **書陵部所蔵資料目録・画像公開システム収録:** 類聚名義抄 (法 (前半部) 残欠・別本) , 宮内庁, 2025.
#    Digital Facsimile available via the system:
#    *(Note: This URL appears to be the viewer link for the specific item described in this entry).*

# We are grateful to the Archives and Mausolea Department of the Imperial Household Agency (Kunai-chō Shoryōbu) for permission to publish the decipherment text of ZRM (Permission No. 宮内発343号, dated March 10, 2014 / 平成26年3月10日). # Consolidated Acknowledgments
